title: Updating Robot Firmware
notes:
Procedure:
1. A hotspot is required. Connect your phone to the hotspot via WiFi.
2. Open the Unitree App (logged in).
3. Navigate to: Device -> Robot Settings -> WiFi.
4. Connect the robot to the hotspot network.
5. In the app, go to: Device -> Update.
6. Download the firmware update (robot battery must be at least 55%).
7. Install the firmware update (robot battery must be at least 30%).
8. Restart the robot after installation.

Current firmware observed:
- Robot updated successfully to version 1.4.7.

Notes:
1. For updating to 1.4.9:
   - Turn on the robot.
   - Enter debug/development mode using L2+B.
   - Connect robot to hotspot WiFi.
   - Start firmware update.

2. Quadraconnect WiFi mode did not work reliably.
   - Personal hotspot connection worked successfully.

Video:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WGIZxfJCnvs

title: Running Arm Control Interface
notes:
SDK location: high level/g1_arm5_sdk_dds_example_cpp

Description:
This interface enables low-level upper body control using user-defined joint-level control (e.g., inverse kinematics) instead of predefined Unitree routines. It can be combined with loco_client for high-level locomotion and low-level manipulation simultaneously.

Reference:
https://support.unitree.com/home/en/G1_developer/arm_control_routine

Procedure:
1. Turn on the robot.
2. Enter ready mode: L2+B, then L2+UP.
3. On Unitree App: Device -> Service Status -> disable g1_arm_example.
4. Run: g1_arm5_dds_example <network_id> (press Enter twice if needed).

Important Notes:
- Do NOT run g1_arm7 version.
- The wrist may collide with thighs if incorrect version is used.
- Robot should be in correct ready state before execution.

Expected Output:
Running_Arm_Control_Interface.png

title: Running Arm Action Interface
notes:
SDK location: high level/g1_arm_action_example_cpp

Description:
This interface controls predefined arm behaviors (e.g., wave, kiss gesture) when the robot is standing upright. The system operates in FSM=801 mode, where the upper body executes Unitree-defined action primitives selected by action IDs.

Reference:
https://support.unitree.com/home/en/G1_developer/arm_action_interface

Procedure:
1. Turn on the robot.
2. Enter ready mode: L2+B, then L2+UP.
3. Lower robot so it is in contact with ground.
4. Switch to standing state: R2 + A.
5. Run: g1_arm_action_example -n <network_id> -i <action_id>.

FSM Requirement:
- Robot must be in FSM 801.
- FSM state can be verified using provided SDK tools (e.g., fsm_id utility).
- If modifying SDK: place code in examples/high_level and update CMakeLists.
- Run check: ./fsm_id <network_name>.

title: High Level Locomotion Control
notes:
SDK location: highlevel/g1_loco_client_example_cpp

Description:
This module provides high-level locomotion control using velocity commands (vx, vy, omega). It enables motion planning and can potentially be combined with arm control interfaces for coordinated full-body behavior.

Reference:
https://support.unitree.com/home/en/G1_developer/rpc_routine

Implementation Notes:
- Modified version exists: g1_loco_template.cpp (local file)
- Located in high_level directory and built via unitree_sdk2

Build & Run:
After adding to CMake, execute from unitree_sdk2/build:
./g1_loco_template --network_interface=<interface> --damp

Example Commands:
- zero_torque
- start
- squat
- sit
- stand_up

FSM Control:
- get_fsm_id
- set_fsm_id=500 or 801

Motion Control:
- continuous_gait=true/false
- set_velocity="0 0 0.2 1"
- move="0 0 0.2"

Local File:
g1_loco_template.cpp

title: Using the Unitree APP (G1 connection and control)
notes:
Connecting to G1 via WiFi (hotspot mode):
1. Turn on the robot.
2. Connect phone WiFi to a hotspot.
3. Open Unitree App: Device -> Robot Settings -> WiFi mode.
4. Enter hotspot name and password.
5. Wait for connection.

Connecting to G1 via AP mode:
1. Turn on the robot.
2. Connect phone WiFi to robot SSID (e.g., G1_6582).
3. Turn OFF cellular data.
4. In Unitree App: Device -> Robot Settings -> AP-mode.
5. Use robot name (e.g., G1_6582) and password (e.g., Legs124&).
6. Wait for connection.

App features after connection:
1. Go: Damping mode -> Preparation -> Walk W. Use joystick for movement (forward, side, turn).
2. Go: Pre-programmed motions (high five, handshake, hug, clap, face wave).
3. BenBen: Available only in WiFi mode.
4. Device: Check battery, motor temperature, calibration.
5. Device -> Service status: Enable/disable robot applications.
